pi_items.source: NASA Science (science.nasa.gov/solar-system/planets/), Cool Cosmos/Caltech (coolcosmos.ipac.caltech.edu), and HowStuffWorks confirm the correct order of 8 planets from the Sun

pi_items.verified_value: Pluto is correctly forbidden as it was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006 by the IAU
pi_items.source: NASA Science (science.nasa.gov/dwarf-planets/pluto/facts/), Library of Congress, and Britannica confirm Pluto was reclassified in 2006 and does not meet the IAU criteria for a full planet

pi_items.verified_value: Sun is correctly forbidden as it is a star, not a planet
pi_items.source: Standard astronomical knowledge - the Sun is the central star of the solar system

pi_items.verified_value: Moon is correctly forbidden as it is Earth's natural satellite, not a planet
pi_items.source: Standard astronomical knowledge - the Moon is a satellite, not a planet

pi_items.verified_value: Ceres is correctly forbidden as it is a dwarf planet in the asteroid belt, not one of the 8 planets
pi_items.source: NASA Science (science.nasa.gov/dwarf-planets/ceres/facts/) and Wikipedia confirm Ceres was classified as a dwarf planet in 2006
Valid Unix/Linux home directory path structure - Documents is a standard user directory

privileged_info_summary: The task requires identifying the correct order of the 8 planets in our solar system: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune (verified from NASA). The forbidden words list is accurate: Pluto is a dwarf planet (reclassified 2006), Ceres is also a dwarf planet in the asteroid belt, Sun is a star, and Moon is Earth's satellite—none are among the 8 official planets. All astronomical data is based on real, verified information from authoritative sources including NASA and the International Astronomical Union.
